question: Will Adriano Espaillat be the Democratic Nominee for NY-13? Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://polymarket.com/market/will-adriano-espaillat-be-the-democratic-nominee-for-ny-13. Background: This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026. If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other". The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/. Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

evidences.title: Adriano Espaillat, Darializa Avila Chevalier to face off in race for NY's 13th Congressional District - ABC7 New York
evidences.content: "I am very used to being out-resourced, outnumbered, and still delivering for my community," she said. New York-13 has become one of the most closely-watched races in this month's primary. The 32-year-old challenger is the daughter of Dominican immigrants, who opposes all deportations, including those of people with criminal convictions. "Many of the folks I've worked with who have served their time are folks that are just trying to rebuild their lives and then are many years later detained on this, and that is a form of double jeopardy," Avila Chevalier said. [...] -- With Primary Day next Tuesday, one of the most contentious races is New York's 13th Congressional District, which includes Upper Manhattan and parts of the West Bronx. For a nearly a decade Congressman Adriano Espaillat has been the political powerhouse for the district. "I'm the first undocumented, formerly undocumented member of Congress," Espaillat said. "I'm the first Dominican American member of Congress in a city that has a million Dominican Americans. But the incumbent is facing a primary challenge from a political newcomer who has support of Mayor Zohran Mamdani, including a high-profile endorsement that r...

Oscar Romero, another candidate in the race who has raised around $11,000, has not picked up much traction. evidences.title: Petition Information | New York State Board of Elections
evidences.content: Cover sheets must contain the following information: Name, residence address, and mailing address if different, of the candidate. The public office or party position sought, including district number, if any. The name of the party or independent body making the nomination. A statement that the petition contains a number of signatures equal to or in excess of the number required by statute. The volume number OR identification number of that volume. The total number of volumes in each petition OR the identification number for each volume of the petition. The following information is optional: [...] Each sheet of the petition must correctly set forth: the date of the election; the name of the candidate and the office or position sought, including district number, if any; the candidate's residence, and if different, their mailing or post office address; information about the signer: date of signing, voter's residence address, town or city and information relating to the person who witnesses the signatures. A designating or independent nominating petition may include a committee on vacancies. Failure to provide such a committee, or naming a committee of fewer than three persons, will...

Your browser does not support the video tag. # Home ## Meet Adriano U.S. Representative Adriano Espaillat proudly represents New York’s Thirteenth Congressional District. Representative Espaillat is the first Dominican American to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives and his congressional district includes Harlem, East Harlem, West Harlem, Hamilton Heights, Washington Heights, Inwood, Marble Hill, and the north-west Bronx. ## stay informed Get E-Mail updates from my office. evidences.content: Early Mail Ballot Tracking Voters can track their early mail ballot via the Board of Elections' Poll Site Search, Voter Registration, and Mail Ballot Tracker available at You may return the ballot in any of the following ways: For the June 23, 2026 Primary Election: 1. Put it in the mail ensuring it receives a postmark no later than June 23rd. Must be received by the County Board of Elections no later than June 30th. 2. Bringing it to your County Board of Elections Office no later than June 23rd by 9pm. 3. Bringing it to an early voting poll site in your county between June 13th and June 21st. 4. Bringing it to a poll site in your county on June 23rd by 9pm. evidences.title: New York City - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Anchored by Wall Street in Manhattan's Financial District, New York City has been called both the world's premier financial and fintech center and the most economically powerful city in the world. As of 2024( the New York metropolitan area is the largest metropolitan economy in the world, with a gross metropolitan product of over US$2.44 trillion. The New York metropolitan area's economy is larger than all but nine countries "List of countries by GDP (nominal)"). Despite having a 24/7 rapid transit system, New York also leads the world in urban automobile traffic congestion. The city is home to the world's two largest stock exchanges by market capitalization of their listed companies: the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq. New York City is an established haven for global investors. [...] Many districts and monuments in New York City are major landmarks, including three of the world's ten most-visited tourist attractions in 2023. A record 66.6 million tourists visited New York City in 2019, bringing in $47.4 billion in tourism revenue. Visitor numbers dropped by two-thirds in 2020 during the pandemic, rebounding to 63.3 million in 2023. Major landmarks in New York City include t...

evidences.title: Become a Poll Worker | New York State Board of Elections
evidences.content: Download ") Scroll back to the top of the page New York State Board of Elections [...] ## Tasks What tasks will you perform as a poll worker? Prepare the polling place for voting Set up the voting equipment Sign-in and process voters Demonstrate voting procedures to the voters Close the polling place Canvass and report the results Assist voter if requested ## Upcoming Poll Dates For the 2026 Primary Election: Early Voting from June 13, 2026 through June 21, 2026 Election Day on June 23, 2026 For the 2026 General Election: Early Voting from October 24, 2026 through November 1, 2026 Election Day on November 3, 2026 ## Compensation Yes, Poll workers get paid for training and each day they work. [...] nygov-logo Election Information # Become a Poll Worker ## Overview The right to vote is one of our nation's oldest and most important entitlements, and with this right comes responsibility. Free and open elections are the basis on which this country was formed and you can make a difference by becoming a poll worker to help protect those freedoms. ## Eligibility In order to be eligible to work as a poll worker, you must be a New York State registered voter. If you are 17 years old, you...
